summary: Matched a batch of item-system functions across Pokeball summons and character/common projectiles, replacing decomp placeholder comments and UNK prototypes with typed C implementations. The PR added logic for Ice Climbers ice animation decay, Mr. Game & Watch chef projectile setup/collision behavior, Lugia and Ho-Oh spawn/flight/effect callbacks, Old Kuri/Goomba state transitions, and Pikachu Thunder state changes. Supporting header work refined item variable and attribute structs so the item code could express real fields instead of broad padding or unknown signatures. The PR body reports all functions were verified as 100% objdiff matches and that ninja built cleanly.
what_changed: Implemented `itClimbersice_UnkMotion0_Anim`, which reads `itClimbersIceAttributes`, scales an item-var float by attribute `x28`, updates a child JObj through `it_80272F7C`, and returns true once the value drops below `0.01F`.

Implemented Mr. Game & Watch chef functions including `it_802C84A0`, `itGamewatchchef_UnkMotion0_Coll`, and `itGamewatchchef_UnkMotion1_Coll`, covering projectile entry selection, velocity setup from chef attributes, wall/ground collision checks via `it_8026DAA8`, bounce reversal, and transition to a stopped animation state.

Implemented Lugia functions `it_2725_Logic17_Spawned`, `itLugia_UnkMotion2_Phys`, and `it_802D1DD8`, including spawn state initialization, vertical velocity accumulation based on `it_80272C6C`, top blast-zone checks through `Stage_GetBlastZoneTopOffset`, and hitlag effect callback setup using `efLib_PauseAll`/`efLib_ResumeAll`.

Implemented Ho-Oh functions `it_2725_Logic18_Spawned`, `itHouou_UnkMotion2_Phys`, `it_802D2B4C`, and `it_802D2C78`, mirroring similar Pokemon item patterns with separate Ho-Oh attributes, itcmd vars, effect spawning on dynamic bones 16 and 17, and hitlag effect callback setup.

Implemented Old Kuri/Goomba functions including `it_802D73F0`, `itOldkuri_UnkMotion2_Anim`, `itOldkuri_UnkMotion2_Coll`, and `itOldkuri_UnkMotion9_Anim`, covering spawn initialization, facing direction selection, velocity reset/setup, ground/air checks, collision-driven transitions, and motion changes.

Implemented Pikachu Thunder state helpers `it_802B211C` and `it_802B22B8`, including transition to motion states, velocity copy from item vars, life timer setup, hit-preserve animation flags, and propagating position/state to a child thunder item when present.
Refined `itCharItems.h` by expanding `itClimbersIce_ItemVars` with `x4` and bitfield `x8_b0`, and adding `itClimbersIceAttributes` with an attribute at offset `0x28`.

Refined `itCommonItems.h` by splitting Ho-Oh padding into typed fields `x8`, `xC`, and `x14`, changing Lugia attribute `x14` from `f32` to `s32`, and exposing `itLugia_ItemVars.x64` as a `Vec3` with adjusted padding.
Updated headers to replace unknown declarations with typed prototypes, including `it_802C84A0(Item_GObj*, s32)`, `it_802D2B4C(Item_GObj*)`, and `it_802D2D2C(Item_GObj*)`.
smart_moves: Converted struct padding only where the implementations needed concrete fields, keeping unknown areas as padding while documenting exact offsets. This is useful in item decomp where large item-var unions are still partially unknown.

Reused shared Pokemon item behavior patterns between Lugia and Ho-Oh: spawn initialization stores starting position, resets itcmd vars/flags, starts a timer, then physics accumulates vertical velocity until the item reaches the top blast-zone boundary.

Preserved matching-sensitive reloads and stack padding, such as repeated `GET_ITEM(gobj)` calls in collision/effect routines and explicit `PAD_STACK(8)`, rather than over-cleaning the C into more idiomatic but non-matching code.

Used existing item engine helpers (`Item_80268E5C`, `it_80275158`, `it_8026DAA8`, `it_8027A09C`, `it_80273454`, `it_80279CDC`) instead of open-coding behavior, making matched code align with established item subsystem conventions.
Tightened prototypes in headers alongside implementation work so future callers can use typed parameters and return types instead of `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS`.
decomp_lessons: For item functions, matching often depends on accurately modeling `xDD4_itemVar` substructures and article `x4_specialAttributes`; promoting specific offsets from padding to typed fields can unlock multiple functions at once.

Do not assume float types for attribute words only because nearby fields are floats. This PR changed Lugia and Ho-Oh timer/count-like fields at offset `0x14` to `s32`, which was necessary for the matched spawn logic.

Pokemon summon functions may share control-flow structure but use different item-var aliases (`lugia`, `pokemon`) and different attribute structs; reuse the pattern, but keep field names and offsets grounded in each item.
Objdiff-matching C may intentionally include non-idiomatic local variable lifetimes, duplicate `GET_ITEM` calls, temporary aliases like `old_ip`/`ip2`, and explicit stack padding.

Collision callbacks commonly return `false` even when they mutate velocity, change animation state, or trigger helper callbacks; return value semantics should be copied from matched behavior, not inferred from side effects.
Header cleanup should be done in tandem with matched code: once a function is implemented, replace `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` with the observed signature to improve future callsite matching.
naming_conventions: Item motion callbacks retain existing project naming with item name plus motion slot and phase, e.g. `itClimbersice_UnkMotion0_Anim`, `itGamewatchchef_UnkMotion1_Coll`, `itLugia_UnkMotion2_Phys`, and `itHouou_UnkMotion2_Phys`.
Unidentified item functions continue to use address-derived names such as `it_802C84A0`, `it_802D1DD8`, `it_802D2B4C`, and `it_802D73F0` until their semantic names are known.
Struct fields without known semantic names use offset-style names (`x4`, `x8_b0`, `x14`, `x64`, `xE50`) and preserve comments with byte offsets.
Boolean bitfields in item vars use suffixes like `x8_b0`, matching the existing convention for bit-level unknown fields.
Header comments preserve ROM/function address annotations such as `/* 2C84A0 */` while updating the C type signature.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `PAD_STACK(8)` in several functions to satisfy stack layout requirements for matching.
Kept duplicate item pointer fetches after calls that may affect compiler aliasing or object state, such as `ip = GET_ITEM(gobj)` or `ip2 = GET_ITEM(gobj)` after helper calls.
Matched float constants exactly in source, including `0.01F`, `0.0F`, `1.0f`, `-1.0f`, `1.3F`, and `5.0f`.
Represented item-var memory as typed structs and vectors where needed, including casting Ho-Oh Pokemon item-var storage to `Vec3*` and promoting Lugia item-var offset `x64` to `Vec3`.
Included additional headers only when implementations required external declarations, such as `gr/stage.h`, `it/it_266F.h`, and `<placeholder.h>`.
Used bitmask checks on collision results (`result & 0xC`, `result & 3`) to match branch behavior in chef projectile collision callbacks.
Relied on project item animation flags such as `ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE`, `ITEM_UNK_0x1`, and `ITEM_HIT_PRESERVE` instead of numeric literals where named flags existed.
